title: Structural Rerun Policy
use_for: deciding when incremental paths must fall back to broad rerun
defining policies for topology or schema changes in a pipeline
separating local change handling from structural rebuild triggers
making full recompute an explicit architectural path instead of an accident
setting thresholds where structural invalidation dominates
do_not_use_for: ordinary local invalidation with no structural change
rules: treat structural rerun as a first-class path, not a failure mode
define what counts as structural change explicitly
prefer broad rerun when local incremental bookkeeping no longer preserves clarity or correctness
separate topology change from ordinary product invalidation
procedure: Define which changes are local and which are structural.
List the triggers that require broad rerun or rebuild.
Specify the fallback path from incremental handling to structural rerun.
Define any thresholds where accumulated local change should escalate to broad rerun.
Verify the policy is cheaper and clearer than forcing brittle fine-grained repair.
output: fallback path from incremental to broad recompute

primary_when: the request is specifically about when a pipeline should stop handling changes locally and rerun broadly
the request is about topology changes, structural invalidation, or explicit full-recompute triggers
must_not_own_when: the request is mainly about ordinary local change batching or stage splitting with no structural fallback question

solves: how to keep broad rerun as an explicit safe path for structural changes
how to stop incremental logic from becoming brittle when topology changes dominate
how to define thresholds where local repair stops being worth it
does_not_solve: ordinary local invalidation rules by themselves
feature-domain semantics outside the pipeline graph

core_principles: A structural rerun path is part of a healthy incremental system, not a design embarrassment.
Topology changes should have their own triggers instead of pretending to be ordinary dirty products.
When local repair becomes too complex, broad rerun is often the clearer architecture.
constraints: Structural triggers must be explicit enough that reviewers can predict when broad recompute occurs.
Fallback to full rerun must preserve correctness even when many local assumptions fail at once.
Thresholds should reflect runtime cost and clarity, not folklore.
decision_rules: Use structural-rerun-policy as the owner when the key problem is deciding when incremental handling should give way to broad recompute.
If topology or schema changes alter many stage relationships, prefer a broad rerun path over brittle partial repair rules.
If local changes accumulate beyond a clear threshold, escalate through an explicit structural trigger rather than letting complexity grow silently.
If broad rerun cost is unacceptable, revisit pipeline architecture or batching rather than hiding the problem inside invalidation logic.

preferred_patterns: explicit separation between local dirty handling and structural rebuild triggers
documented thresholds for escalation to broad rerun
clean fallback from incremental path to full recompute
avoid_patterns: pretending every structural change can be repaired locally
broad reruns triggered implicitly by obscure side effects
full recompute with no named trigger policy
incremental complexity growing until no fallback remains

recipes.name: Define structural rebuild triggers
recipes.when: Use when a pipeline supports local reruns but some changes affect too much of the graph to patch cleanly.
steps: List topology, schema, or ownership changes that invalidate broad structure.
Map those changes to a deliberate full-rerun trigger.
Keep the broad-rerun path separate from ordinary local change handling.
Measure whether thresholds and triggers stay understandable under load.

pitfalls: Treating broad rerun as a hidden failure path instead of an explicit design choice.
Allowing structural invalidation to masquerade as ordinary local dirtiness.
Accumulating increasingly complex repair logic just to avoid admitting the need for broad rerun.
review_checklist: Is the fallback to broad rerun clear and correct?
Are topology changes separated from ordinary local invalidation?
Are escalation thresholds understandable and measurable?

references.title: Local Reference: Structural Rerun Policy
references.interpretation: Use this first for full-recompute triggers, topology-aware invalidation, and explicit fallback from local rerun to broad rebuild.
references.why: Use as the primary local guide for structural invalidation and broad-rerun policy.

references.title: Local Reference: Incremental Recompute
references.interpretation: Use this to keep structural fallback aligned with the existing local change and retained-product model.
references.why: Use to connect structural rerun rules with the domain's incremental path.
